title: Basic inheritance
instruction:
Use `: BaseClass` to inherit.

Run this.
successMessage: Dog inherits Name and Breathe() from Animal, then adds Bark(). Inheritance models 'is-a' relationships: a Dog IS an Animal.

title: virtual and override
instruction:
`virtual` marks a base method as overridable. `override` replaces it in the derived class.

Run this.
successMessage: Even though s is typed as Shape, s.Area() calls Circle.Area() — runtime polymorphism. Describe() uses the overridden Area() automatically.

title: base keyword
instruction:
`base` calls the base class constructor or method.

Run this.
successMessage: base(name) passes the argument to Animal's constructor. base.Speak() calls the original method and Cat appends to it.

title: Abstract classes
instruction:
`abstract` methods have no implementation — subclasses must provide one.

Run this.
successMessage: Abstract classes cannot be instantiated directly. They define a contract that concrete subclasses must fulfil. Print() uses the abstract Area() — polymorphism.

title: sealed keyword
instruction:
`sealed` prevents further inheritance. `sealed override` prevents further overriding.

Run this.
successMessage: sealed prevents subclassing. Sealing a class can also be a performance hint to the JIT compiler for devirtualisation.

title: is and as operators
instruction:
`is` checks the type. `as` casts to a type (returns null on failure instead of throwing).

Run this.
successMessage: 'a is Dog d' checks if a is a Dog AND binds it to d. This is pattern matching — clean and safe type casting.

title: Polymorphic collections
instruction:
A `List<Animal>` can hold any Animal subclass.

Run this.
successMessage: The list holds Animals but each Sound() call dispatches to the correct subclass at runtime. This is the power of polymorphism.

title: Object as universal base class
instruction:
Every C# class implicitly inherits from `object` (System.Object).

Run this.
successMessage: object is the root of all C# types. Any value can be stored in an object variable (boxing for value types). GetType() returns the actual runtime type.
